channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: After 12 failed attempts over 2 years, Lebanon's parliament will try to pick a president
item.description: Lebanon’s parliament is set to vote to elect a president to fill a more than an two-year-long presidential vacuum.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An Italian journalist is freed from detention in Iran and returns home
item.description: ROME (AP) — An Italian journalist detained in Iran for three weeks was freed Wednesday and returned home, after her fate had become intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer arrested in Italy and wanted by the United States.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A Russian missile attack in southern Ukraine has killed at least 13 civilians, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A daytime Russian missile attack on the southern Ukraine city of Zaporizhzhia killed at least 13 civilians and wounded about 30 others on Wednesday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon 'not aware of any plans' to take Greenland by force
item.description: The Pentagon on Wednesday said it was “not aware” of any plans to invade Greenland if ordered to do so, after President-elect Trump a day earlier refused to rule out using military force to seize Denmark's autonomous territory. “I'm certainly not going to get into hypothetical situations.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Venezuela's ultimate political survivor Nicolás Maduro faces his toughest challenge yet
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro is facing more international rebuke than at any time in his 12 years in power. The self-declared socialist is widely believed to have lost last year's election by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US troops need to stay in Syria to counter the Islamic State group, Austin says
item.description: Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin told The Associated Press that the U.S. needs to keep troops deployed in Syria to prevent the Islamic State group from reconstituting as a major threat.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gunshots are heard near Chad's presidential palace. Foreign minister says it's under control
item.description: N’DJAMENA, Chad (AP) — Multiple gunshots were heard near the presidential palace in Chad's capital Wednesday evening, residents said, while the African country's foreign minister said soldiers were defending the president and the situation was under control.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More Americans jailed in Venezuela pose a test of Trump's deal-making foreign policy
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro will be sworn in for a third term Friday while hundreds of government opponents arrested since his disputed reelection last summer languish in the country's packed prisons. Sharing cells with them are as many as 10 Americans.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Panama Canal chief dismisses Trump’s claims on China, fees
item.description: The Panama Canal Authority chief dismissed President-elect Trump’s claims that China is controlling the trade route and criticized his comments about fees. Ricaurte Vásquez Morales told The Wall Street Journal that Trump’s accusations that China is running the Panama Canal are “unfounded.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader rallies support in Panama ahead of Maduro inauguration
item.description: Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González is rallying regional support for the opposition's claims that he won the July election against President Nicolás Maduro.
item.dc:creator: Juan Zamorano And Astrid Suárez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bolsonaro says he's requested passport's return to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: Brazil’s former president, Jair Bolsonaro, officially requested his passport from Minister Alexandre de Moraes on Wednesday so he may attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Canadian leaders say Trump's talk about Canada becoming the 51st state isn't funny anymore
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — U.S. President-elect Donald Trump’s comments that Canada should become the 51st state are no longer a joke and are meant to undermine America’s closest ally, Canada’s finance minister said Wednesday. Dominic LeBlanc, the country’s point person for U.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gingrich: Trump 'trying to project' strength with Greenland talk
item.description: Former Speaker Newt Gingrich (R-Ga.) says President-elect Trump is attempting to project strength with his recent remarks about the U.S. acquiring Greenland from Denmark, comments that have not been well-received across the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: Tristan Lejeune, The Hill

item.title: Israeli Army says it recovered body of hostage in Gaza
item.description: The Israeli Army on Wednesday said it had recovered the body from the Gaza Strip of an Israeli citizen who was kidnapped by Hamas during its terrorist attack on Oct. 7 and held hostage, and was working to identify the remains of another body as a possible Israeli hostage.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump expected to appoint Eric Trager as Middle East director for National Security Council
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to announce Eric Trager as Senior Director for the Middle East and North Africa on the National Security Council, a congressional aide told The Hill. Trager has served as a professional staff member for Republicans on the Senate Armed Services Committee since 2018.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Mexico offers protection to famed singer after drug cartel death threats
item.description: Mexican authorities are offering protection to famed regional Mexican singer Natanael Cano and other artists after a drug cartel publicly threatened them, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 9 miners are trapped in a coal mine in India's northeastern Assam state
item.description: Indian authorities have summoned the army to help rescue at least nine workers who are trapped inside a flooded coal mine in the country's northeastern Assam state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump reinforces 'all hell will break out' if hostages not returned by inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump once again threatened "all hell will break out" if the hostages in Gaza are not freed by the time he enters office, though he refused to detail what actions he will take.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada's Liberal Party in search of a new leader while dealing with Trump threats, pending election
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's Liberal Party finds itself in search of a new leader while dealing with U.S. President-elect Donald Trump's threats to impose steep tariffs on Canadian goods and with Canada's election just months away.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Migrants left stranded in troubled resort as Mexico disperses them far from US border
item.description: ACAPULCO, Mexico (AP) — About 100 migrants from various countries wandered directionless and disoriented through the streets of the troubled Pacific coast resort of Acapulco.
item.dc:creator: ANTONIO CASTILLO and MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bangladesh's ailing former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia to undergo medical treatment in London
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Bangladesh’s ailing former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia was traveling to London on Tuesday for medical treatment, her aides said, leaving behind a nation grappling with uncertainty over its political future after her archrival, former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina, was ousted i
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Earthquake 50 miles from Mount Everest leaves at least 126 dead in Tibet
item.description: A powerful magnitude 7.1 earthquake has struck Tibet, reportedly killing at least 126 on the Chinese side of the border and damaging more than 1,000 homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Afghans land in the Philippines for visa processing ahead of resettlement in US
item.description: A group of Afghan nationals arrived in the Philippines for the processing of special immigrant visas in order to resettle in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congo to execute over 170 people convicted of armed robbery, official says
item.description: Over 170 inmates have been flown from the Democratic Republic of the Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to a high-security prison where they will be executed, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Pierre Poilievre? Canada's Conservative leader seeking to become next prime minister after Trudeau exit
item.description: The leader of Canada's Conservative party, Pierre Poilievre, has been leading in the polls and is favorite to become the country's next prime minister following the exit of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
